#  Archive and Unarchive Cost Codes Synced with an ERP Integration 
##### ERP DOCUMENTATION
**Each Procore-built ERP integration offers its own unique feature set.** Your integration might not support the action described on this page.
To learn about your integration and its supported features, see [Which ERP integrations are supported by Procore?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-which-erp-integrations-are-supported-by-procore)
##  Background 
If you do not want unused cost codes to appear in projects, you can "archive" the unneeded cost codes. This can result in a cleaner Procore interface by removing cost codes that you do not need to work with. Archiving a cost code from the ERP Standard Cost Code list means the cost code can not be added to new or existing projects on which the cost code does not already exist. Archived cost codes can be “unarchived”, making the cost codes again available to be added to new or existing projects.
##  Things to Consider 
  * **Required User Permissions** :
    * 'Standard' or 'Admin' level permissions on the company's Admin tool.
  * **Additional Information:**
    * Archiving a cost code does not impact the cost code on any existing projects where it already exists. The feature is only available on ERP-synced Standard Cost Code lists. This feature applies to companies integrated with an Integrated ERP System.
  * Not all ERP integrations handle cost codes in the same way. 


##  Steps 
  * Archive Cost Codes
  * Unarchive Cost Codes


### Archive Cost Codes
  1. Navigate to the company's **Admin** tool.
  2. Under **Company Settings** , click **Work Breakdown Structure**.
  3. Select **Cost Codes**.
  4. Select the cost code list from **View - ERP Standard Cost Code List**.
  5. From the **Status** column, mark the **Cost Code** check box, then select **Inactive** from the drop-down menu.  
_Note: Cost codes that are archived will appear in red in the list._


### Unarchive Cost Codes
  1. Navigate to the company's **Admin** tool.
  2. Under **Company Settings** , click **Work Breakdown Structure**.
  3. Select **Cost Codes**.
  4. Select the cost code list from **View - ERP Standard Cost Code List**.
  5. From the **Status** column, mark the **Cost Code** check box, then select **Active** from the drop-down menu.  
_Note: Cost codes that are archived will appear in red in the list._


## See Also
  * [Add the 'ERP Direct Costs' Column to a Procore Budget View for ERP Integrations](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/admin-company/tutorials/add-the-erp-direct-costs-column-to-a-procore-budget-view-for-erp-integrations)


In Procore, the term _integrated ERP System_ , is used to refer to _Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP)_ software applications that can be configured to exchange data between Procore and a supported application using the Company level ERP Integrations tool. See [Which ERP integrations are supported by Procore?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-which-erp-integrations-are-supported-by-procore)
title: Archive and Unarchive Cost Codes Synced with an ERP Integration
description: To archive and unarchive ERP-synced cost codes using the Company Admin tool.
